threats.title: Retroactive Gauge Weight Application via epochTime Bug in update_market
threats.hypothesis_description: In update_market, epochTime is computed using block.number (current block) but applied to ALL historical block iterations. gauge_relative_weight_write(_market, epochTime) therefore returns the CURRENT gauge weight for every historical epoch. An attacker can inflate a market's gauge weight via voting, then trigger update_market to retroactively apply the inflated weight to all un-updated historical epochs, claiming massively inflated CANTO rewards.
threats.investigation_rationale: The variable `i` iterates through historical blocks, but epochTime is fixed at current block's estimated time. This means the gauge weight lookup always returns the current-period weight instead of the weight active during each historical epoch. This is a direct code bug with material economic impact.

proof_of_signal.pattern: epochTime = referenceBlockTime + ((block.number - referenceBlockNumber) * averageBlockTime) / 1000 — uses block.number instead of loop variable i for all iterations

preconditions: Market hasn't had update_market called for multiple epochs (inactive market)
Attacker has veCANTO to vote on gauge weights
Attacker has deposits in the target market during un-updated epochs
cantoPerBlock is set for the historical epochs

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: epochTime uses block.number (constant for all loop iterations) instead of i (the historical block being processed)
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Vote to inflate gauge weight → call update_market → current weight applied retroactively to all historical blocks since lastRewardBlock
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: If market had 5% relative weight historically but attacker inflates to 50%, all un-updated epochs yield 10x more rewards than deserved

economic_questions: How many epochs typically go without update_market being called on low-activity markets?
What is the capital cost of veCANTO needed to meaningfully shift gauge weights?
Can the attack be executed atomically (vote + update_market + claim in sequence)?
validation_requirements: Confirm gauge_relative_weight_write returns current-period weight when called with current epochTime
Calculate profit: (new_weight - old_weight) * cantoPerBlock * total_un_updated_blocks * attacker_share / marketSupply
Test with a market that hasn't been updated for 5+ epochs

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: veCANTO lock sufficient to swing gauge weight (protocol-dependent)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: ~0.01 ETH in gas for vote + update_market + claim
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Proportional to un-updated epochs × weight differential × cantoPerBlock
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Up to 10x reward multiplier on historical epochs (requires validation)
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Multi-transaction: lock CANTO, vote, then trigger update+claim

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: CANTO to lock for veCANTO; market deposit for reward eligibility
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When target market has maximum backlog of un-updated epochs
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Steals emissions from other markets; no direct price impact

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All depositors in other markets (reduced reward share)
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional to how much weight was redirected retroactively
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All CANTO rewards for the un-updated epoch backlog

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: cantoPerBlock values, typical market update frequency, gauge weight distribution
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Compare epochTime using block.number vs i; verify different gauge weights returned

threats.title: remove_gauge Always Reverts Due to gauge_types_ Zeroed Before _remove_gauge_weight
threats.hypothesis_description: In remove_gauge, gauge_types_[_gauge] is set to 0 BEFORE calling _remove_gauge_weight. Inside _remove_gauge_weight, gauge_type = gauge_types_[_gauge] - 1 = -1, causing _get_sum(-1) to return 0. Then new_sum = 0 - old_weight_bias underflows for any gauge with positive weight, reverting the transaction. Governance permanently loses the ability to remove gauges, meaning malicious or deprecated gauges cannot be deactivated and continue receiving CANTO emissions forever.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a definitive ordering bug. The function sets gauge_types_ to 0 at line ~197, then _remove_gauge_weight reads it at line ~210. Any governance attempt to remove a gauge will fail, creating a permanent governance DoS.

proof_of_signal.pattern: gauge_types_[_gauge] = 0 on line 195, then _remove_gauge_weight(_gauge) on line 196 reads gauge_types_[_gauge] - 1 = -1

preconditions: Gauge has been added with non-zero weight or has any active votes
Governance attempts to call remove_gauge

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: gauge_types_[_gauge] = 0; then int128 gauge_type = gauge_types_[_gauge] - 1 = -1; _get_sum(-1) returns 0; new_sum = 0 - old_weight_bias underflows
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Malicious gauge added by governance can never be removed; continues receiving emission share indefinitely
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Irremovable gauge captures emissions perpetually, draining rewards from legitimate gauges

economic_questions: Can governance use remove_gauge_weight as a workaround (doesn't zero gauge_types_)?
What is the economic impact of an irremovable gauge on total emission distribution?
Can users still vote for removed gauges if gauge_types_ remains non-zero?
validation_requirements: Confirm remove_gauge reverts for any gauge with old_weight_bias > 0
Test remove_gauge_weight as alternative path
Verify if changes_sum[-1] subtraction also causes reverts even for zero-bias gauges

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: None for exploit; gauge was already added by governance
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: N/A - the bug prevents governance action
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Continuous emission capture by irremovable gauge
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: N/A - governance DoS, not direct profit extraction

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Always exploitable once a gauge is added
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Governance cannot respond to malicious or deprecated markets

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All other gauge depositors who lose emission share
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional to irremovable gauge's weight share
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All future CANTO emissions directed to irremovable gauges

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Gauge weight of target gauge, total gauge weight
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace remove_gauge execution path with concrete values
investigation_priorities.execution_complexity: N/A - this is a code defect preventing intended functionality

threats.title: uint128 Truncation in accCantoPerShare Corrupts Reward Accounting for Low-Supply Markets
threats.hypothesis_description: accCantoPerShare and secRewardsPerShare are stored as uint128. The formula (blockDelta * cantoPerBlock * gaugeRelativeWeight) / marketSupply can exceed uint128 max (~3.4e38) when marketSupply is very small. Solidity 0.8 explicit uint128() cast silently truncates higher bits, corrupting the accumulated reward value. An attacker who is the first/only depositor with 1 wei can cause accCantoPerShare to wrap, either destroying other users' rewards or enabling extraction of incorrect reward amounts.
threats.investigation_rationale: With marketSupply=1, cantoPerBlock=1e18, relativeWeight=1e18, a single block adds 1e36 to accCantoPerShare. After ~340 blocks, the accumulation exceeds uint128 max. The uint128() cast truncates silently, corrupting all subsequent reward calculations.

proof_of_signal.pattern: uint128() cast on (blockDelta * cantoPerBlock[epoch] * gauge_relative_weight_write) / marketSupply — no overflow check before narrowing cast

preconditions: Newly whitelisted market with no minimum deposit requirement
cantoPerBlock > 0 for current epoch
Attacker is first depositor and deposits 1 wei
Multiple blocks pass before another deposit

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: marketSupply=1, cantoPerBlock=1e18, weight=1e18 → per-block accumulation = 1e36. uint128 max = 3.4e38. Overflow after ~340 blocks.
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: First depositor with 1 wei waits for overflow, then new depositors have corrupted accCantoPerShare baseline, enabling incorrect reward extraction
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: After truncation, rewardDebt for new depositors is based on garbage accCantoPerShare; attacker's claim may yield inflated or deflated rewards depending on truncated value

economic_questions: Does uint128() in Solidity 0.8.17 revert or truncate on overflow?
If truncation: what is the resulting accCantoPerShare value and how does it affect subsequent claims?
Can the attacker predict the truncated value and position for profit?
validation_requirements: Test uint128 cast behavior in Solidity 0.8.17 with values exceeding max
Calculate exact truncated values for typical cantoPerBlock settings
Simulate full deposit→wait→overflow→newDeposit→claim flow

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: ~0.01 ETH gas for deposit and claim
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Depends on truncated value behavior; could brick market or enable reward theft
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Requires validation of truncation vs revert behavior

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after market whitelisting, before other deposits

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All future depositors in the market
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete loss of rewards or inability to interact with market
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All CANTO rewards allocated to the affected market

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Exact cantoPerBlock values, typical market supply levels
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test uint128 narrowing cast behavior empirically

threats.title: Permanently Locked Voting Power Due to Contradictory Requires in vote_for_gauge_weights
threats.hypothesis_description: vote_for_gauge_weights allows 0-weight votes on non-gauges (gauge_types_==0) via the first require, intending to let users reclaim power from removed gauges. However, the later require(gauge_type >= 0) always fails when gauge_types_==0 (since gauge_type = 0-1 = -1). This contradictory logic means users who voted for a removed gauge can NEVER reclaim their allocated basis points, permanently reducing their voting capacity.
threats.investigation_rationale: The two require statements create a logical contradiction: the first allows the operation, the second blocks it. Any voting power allocated to a gauge that is later removed (gauge_types_ set to 0 via any path) becomes permanently locked in vote_user_power.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Line 243: require(_user_weight == 0 || gauge_types_[_gauge_addr] != 0) allows 0-weight on removed gauges. Line 259: require(gauge_type >= 0) blocks when gauge_types_ == 0 since gauge_type = 0-1 = -1

preconditions: User has voted for a gauge (vote_user_slopes[user][gauge].power > 0)
The gauge's gauge_types_ is set to 0 (via successful remove_gauge or any other path)
User attempts to reclaim voting power by voting 0

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: After gauge removal, user's vote_user_power permanently includes the locked allocation, reducing their effective voting capacity
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Affected users lose governance influence proportional to their locked bps; competing voters gain disproportionate influence over emissions

economic_questions: Since remove_gauge itself reverts (ECON-H002), can gauge_types_ ever become 0?
Is there any alternative path that sets gauge_types_ to 0?
What is the maximum voting power loss per affected user?
validation_requirements: Confirm the contradictory requires by calling vote_for_gauge_weights(removed_gauge, 0)
Check if remove_gauge can succeed under any conditions (zero weight, zero slope)
Test vote_user_power accumulation after gauge removal

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: None for attacker; this is a code defect affecting users
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect: competing voters gain outsized influence over CANTO emissions
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Proportional to locked bps across all affected users

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Any user who voted for a subsequently removed gauge
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Up to 10000 bps (100%) of voting power permanently locked
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All affected users' governance influence over CANTO emissions

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Number of active gauge voters, typical bps allocation patterns
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test full sequence: vote → remove_gauge → attempt reclaim

threats.title: LiquidityGauge burn() Permanently Locks Underlying Tokens
threats.hypothesis_description: LiquidityGauge inherits ERC20Burnable, exposing public burn() and burnFrom(). Calling burn() destroys gauge tokens and triggers _afterTokenTransfer → sync_ledger (reducing the user's ledger balance), but does NOT transfer underlying tokens back to the user. The underlying tokens remain permanently locked in the gauge contract with no recovery mechanism.
threats.investigation_rationale: ERC20Burnable is a standard interface that wallets and dApps may present as a normal operation. The burn path is a one-way destruction of value since withdrawUnderlying is the only function that returns underlying tokens. Documented invariant states gauge should be 1:1 backed, which is violated after burn.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Inherits ERC20Burnable which exposes public burn(uint256) and burnFrom(address,uint256). _afterTokenTransfer handles sync_ledger update but no override of burn to return underlying.

preconditions: User holds LiquidityGauge tokens
User calls burn() instead of withdrawUnderlying()

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: burn() destroys gauge tokens; _afterTokenTransfer calls sync_ledger(user, -amount); underlying tokens have no transfer path in burn flow
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Accidental or deliberate call to standard ERC20 burn function causes permanent loss
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Remaining gauge totalSupply < underlying balance; last users can't withdraw; excess underlying is unrecoverable

economic_questions: Can a griefing attacker burn others' tokens via burnFrom with allowance?
Is there any admin recovery mechanism for stuck underlying?
Could a smart contract integrator inadvertently call burn instead of withdrawUnderlying?
validation_requirements: Confirm burn() doesn't transfer underlying
Verify _afterTokenTransfer properly decrements ledger balance
Test that underlying tokens are truly unrecoverable after burn

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Cost of underlying tokens deposited and then burned
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: N/A - this is a fund loss vector, not a profit extraction

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Permanent loss of underlying for burned amount

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: User who calls burn (self-harm) or approver victim of burnFrom
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Up to full gauge underlying balance

threats.title: De-Whitelisting Market Permanently Freezes User Funds in LiquidityGauge
threats.hypothesis_description: If governance de-whitelists a market, update_market reverts with 'Market not whitelisted'. LiquidityGauge.withdrawUnderlying calls _burn which triggers _afterTokenTransfer → sync_ledger → update_market, which reverts. All LiquidityGauge functions (withdraw, transfer, transferFrom) trigger _afterTokenTransfer, so ALL user tokens become permanently frozen with no recovery path.
threats.investigation_rationale: The _afterTokenTransfer hook creates a hard dependency on update_market succeeding. De-whitelisting breaks this dependency, creating an irreversible DoS. Users cannot withdraw underlying tokens even though the underlying tokens are physically in the gauge contract.

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(lendingMarketWhitelist[_market], 'Market not whitelisted') in update_market; called on every gauge token transfer via _afterTokenTransfer → sync_ledger

preconditions: Market has active LiquidityGauge with user deposits
Governance de-whitelists the market (sets lendingMarketWhitelist to false)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: update_market require(whitelist) → revert propagates through sync_ledger → _afterTokenTransfer → _burn → withdrawUnderlying
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Governance action (even well-intentioned) permanently locks all user funds in the gauge
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit extraction; total loss of deposited underlying tokens for all users in the gauge

economic_questions: Is there any way for governance to restore access after de-whitelisting?
Can governance re-whitelist and then allow withdrawals?
What is the total value potentially locked across all gauge markets?
validation_requirements: Confirm withdrawUnderlying reverts when market is de-whitelisted
Confirm transfers also revert (total freeze)
Test re-whitelisting as recovery path and verify reward accounting integrity

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Total lockup of all underlying tokens in affected gauge

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All users with deposits in the de-whitelisted market's gauge
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire TVL of the affected LiquidityGauge

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Number of gauge-enabled markets, TVL per gauge
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test full de-whitelist → withdrawUnderlying revert sequence

threats.title: unlockOverride Enables Infinite Voting Power Cycling Attack
threats.hypothesis_description: When unlockOverride is enabled, users can withdraw locked CANTO early. However, GaugeController is NOT notified of the withdrawal — the user's gauge votes remain active until the original 5-year lock expiration. An attacker can: lock CANTO → vote for gauge → withdraw early → transfer CANTO to new address → lock again → vote again, multiplying gauge weight with a fixed capital amount.
threats.investigation_rationale: VotingEscrow.withdraw zeroes the lock and checkpoints VE state, but GaugeController's vote_user_slopes, vote_user_power, and gauge weight points remain unchanged. The stale votes persist and decay based on the original lock_end, not the actual withdrawal. No cross-contract notification exists.

proof_of_signal.pattern: withdraw() zeroes locked[msg.sender] and checkpoints VE, but no call to GaugeController to invalidate votes; GaugeController still has active vote_user_slopes for the user

preconditions: unlockOverride is enabled by governance
Attacker has CANTO to lock
Attacker can create multiple addresses (or use one address cyclically)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: After withdraw: locked[user].amount=0 but vote_user_slopes[user][gauge] still active with original slope and end time
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Each cycle adds the full veCANTO slope to gauge weight; N cycles = N× gauge weight from fixed capital

economic_questions: How frequently has unlockOverride been toggled historically?
What is the maximum gauge weight inflation achievable per cycle?
Can the attacker profit by directing inflated emissions to their own market position?
validation_requirements: Confirm GaugeController votes persist after VE withdrawal
Test lock→vote→withdraw→relock→vote cycle and measure gauge weight
Calculate emissions captured per cycle vs capital cost

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Any amount of CANTO (recycled across cycles)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Gas for lock+vote+withdraw per cycle (~0.05 ETH)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Linear in number of cycles: N cycles = N× normal gauge influence
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Unbounded multiplier on effective voting power
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: As many cycles as gas budget allows

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Fixed amount of CANTO, reused across cycles
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete capture of gauge emissions by attacker-favored markets

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional dilution of their gauge influence
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All CANTO emissions for affected epochs

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Simulate multi-cycle attack and measure gauge weight inflation

threats.title: Block Time Drift Causes epochTime to Exceed block.timestamp, Zeroing All Gauge Weights
threats.hypothesis_description: epochTime in update_market is estimated using averageBlockTime and referenceBlockNumber. If actual block production is faster than estimated, epochTime drifts ahead of block.timestamp. GaugeController._get_total only fills points_total up to block.timestamp. When epochTime exceeds block.timestamp by >1 week, gauge_relative_weight_write looks up an unfilled future week where points_total=0, returning weight=0. All market rewards silently become zero.
threats.investigation_rationale: The Canto chain block time may fluctuate. A 3-4% drift over months causes epochTime to lead block.timestamp by a full week, at which point all gauge weights read as 0. This silently destroys all CANTO reward distributions until governance manually corrects the block time parameters.
threats.config_evidence: Deploy.sol sets no block time parameters; defaults in LendingLedger constructor: averageBlockTime=5700, referenceBlockNumber=block.number, referenceBlockTime=block.timestamp

proof_of_signal.pattern: epochTime = referenceBlockTime + ((block.number - referenceBlockNumber) * averageBlockTime) / 1000 — no validation that epochTime <= block.timestamp; GaugeController returns 0 weight for future timestamps

preconditions: Actual average block time is faster than the configured averageBlockTime
Sufficient blocks have passed for drift to exceed one week (~3+ months at 3% drift)
Governance has not called setBlockTimeParameters to correct the drift

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: If actual=5.5s, configured=5.7s → drift=0.2s/block → after 3M blocks (~6 months), drift = 600,000s ≈ 1 week → gauge weights return 0
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Natural block time variation silently zeros all reward distributions
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit; all users lose rewards for affected epochs; recovery requires governance intervention

economic_questions: What is the actual Canto chain block time variance?
How quickly would drift accumulate to exceed one week?
Are there monitoring mechanisms to detect the drift?
validation_requirements: Compare estimated epochTime vs actual block.timestamp at various block counts
Test gauge_relative_weight_write return value when time > block.timestamp
Verify that once drift occurs, all markets simultaneously lose rewards

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: N/A - natural occurrence, not direct attack
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: N/A - loss event, not profit extraction

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After months of uncorrected block time drift
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Total reward distribution failure across all markets

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All liquidity providers across all markets
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All CANTO emissions during the drift period

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Canto chain actual block times, averageBlockTime=5700
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Simulate epochTime drift over realistic timeframes

threats.title: _remove_gauge_weight Underflow in points_sum Slope Subtraction Can Brick Gauge Type
threats.hypothesis_description: In _remove_gauge_weight (called via remove_gauge_weight by governance), the line `points_sum[gauge_type][next_time].slope -= old_weight_slope` has no underflow protection. If multiple gauges of the same type are removed, or if slope accounting drifts due to the fill-in mechanism in _get_sum, this subtraction can underflow and revert. Additionally, `changes_sum[gauge_type][time_to_check] -= gauge_weight_change` in the cleanup loop also lacks underflow protection. A revert in _get_sum or _remove_gauge_weight would brick all operations for that gauge type.
threats.investigation_rationale: vote_for_gauge_weights has explicit underflow protection for changes_sum/changes_weight subtractions, but _remove_gauge_weight does not. This asymmetry suggests the removal path was not as carefully implemented. If _get_sum reverts due to corrupted slope data, it blocks _get_total, which blocks all gauge weight operations.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Line 221: points_sum[gauge_type][next_time].slope -= old_weight_slope without underflow check. Line 230: changes_sum[gauge_type][time_to_check] -= gauge_weight_change without underflow check. Compare with vote_for_gauge_weights lines 297-304 which have explicit >= checks.

preconditions: Governance calls remove_gauge_weight for a gauge
The gauge's old_weight_slope > points_sum[type][next_time].slope (possible after delayed _get_sum fill or concurrent operations)
OR: changes_sum[type][t] < gauge_weight_change at some future time t

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: points_sum[gauge_type][next_time].slope -= old_weight_slope — no underflow check, can revert in Solidity 0.8
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: If _get_sum hasn't been called recently, points_sum at next_time might not be up to date, causing the slope to be less than old_weight_slope
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: DoS on the entire gauge type; all gauges of that type become non-functional for voting and weight distribution

economic_questions: Under what conditions does points_sum slope become less than an individual gauge's slope?
Can concurrent voting and removal operations create the underflow condition?
What fraction of gauge types would be affected?
validation_requirements: Test remove_gauge_weight after delayed _get_sum updates
Simulate multiple gauge removals from same type
Verify if _get_sum catch-up prevents the underflow in all cases

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: N/A - DoS vector, not profit extraction
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Permanent if _get_sum is bricked for the type

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When governance removes gauge after period of no checkpoints

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All markets/gauges of the affected type
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete loss of voting and emission direction for that type
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All emissions allocated to the bricked gauge type

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Number of gauge types, gauges per type, checkpoint frequency
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Compare slope values before and after _get_sum fill-in

threats.title: VotingEscrow Checkpoint Loop (255 Weeks) Falls Short of LOCKTIME (260 Weeks)
threats.hypothesis_description: The _checkpoint and _supplyAt functions iterate at most 255 weeks to fill global point history. However, LOCKTIME = 1825 days ≈ 260.7 weeks. If no checkpoint is called for more than 255 weeks, the loop cannot fully process all pending slope changes, leaving 5+ weeks of slope changes unprocessed. This results in incorrect totalSupply and voting power calculations, affecting gauge weight distribution and reward allocation.
threats.investigation_rationale: The constant 255 is hardcoded in both _checkpoint and _supplyAt loops. Since locks last 260+ weeks, there is a 5-week window where slope changes could be missed if checkpoints are delayed. This violates the documented assumption that checkpoints must be called within 5 years.

proof_of_signal.pattern: for (uint256 i = 0; i < 255; i++) — 255 iterations × 1 WEEK = 255 weeks < LOCKTIME (1825 days ≈ 261 weeks)

preconditions: No checkpoint called for > 255 weeks (~4.9 years)
Active locks with slope changes scheduled beyond 255 weeks from last checkpoint

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: 255 weeks < 260.7 weeks (LOCKTIME/WEEK); 5-week gap where slope changes may be skipped
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: If the 5-week gap is reached, global totalSupply becomes incorrect, affecting all gauge weight calculations
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Inflated or deflated totalSupply affects gauge_relative_weight calculations in GaugeController and reward distributions in LendingLedger

economic_questions: How likely is a 255-week checkpoint gap on the Canto chain?
What is the magnitude of totalSupply error from 5 weeks of missed slope changes?
Does the error benefit any specific user or market?
validation_requirements: Calculate maximum voting power error from 5 weeks of unprocessed slope changes
Simulate checkpoint after 260 weeks of inactivity
Verify that subsequent checkpoints can recover the missed slope changes

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Depends on magnitude of slope changes in the 5-week gap
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Likely small unless many locks expire in that window
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Requires 4.9+ years of no VE interaction

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Incorrect totalSupply affecting all gauge weight ratios

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All veCANTO holders and emission recipients
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Small unless significant slope changes occur in the gap
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Proportional to voting power changes in the 5-week window

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: LOCKTIME=1825 days, WEEK=7 days, loop limit=255
